description: SEC 10-K Autonomous Risk Analyzer — Multi-agent pipeline with LangGraph + LangChain + LangSmith

phases.name: Phase 1a: Project Setup & Environment
phases.description: Initialize the project scaffold: create requirements.txt with all core dependencies (langchain, langgraph, langsmith, chromadb, pinecone-client, sentence-transformers, streamlit, sec-edgar-downloader), create .env.example with all required keys and mode flags (USE_LOCAL_LLM, USE_LOCAL_VECTORDB), and create the src/ directory structure. Verify the .venv activates cleanly and all packages import without error.
acceptance_criteria: All packages install from requirements.txt without conflicts
.env.example documents every required env var with inline comments
src/ directory exists with __init__.py
No import errors when running: python -c 'import langchain, langgraph, langsmith, chromadb, streamlit'

phases.name: Phase 1b: Data Ingestion Pipeline (EDGAR → ChromaDB)
phases.description: Implement src/ingestion.py with a factory pattern. The module must: (1) download a 10-K from the SEC EDGAR API for a given ticker using sec-edgar-downloader, extracting the 'Risk Factors' section; (2) split the text with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter (chunk_size=1000, overlap=200); (3) embed with HuggingFaceEmbeddings (all-MiniLM-L6-v2); (4) store in ChromaDB if USE_LOCAL_VECTORDB=true, or Pinecone if false. Export a get_vectorstore(ticker: str) -> VectorStore factory function; (5) implement validate_ticker(ticker: str) -> tuple[bool, str] to check ticker validity and resolve it to a company name. All external API calls must be mocked in tests.
acceptance_criteria: get_vectorstore('AAPL') returns a populated ChromaDB collection in local mode
get_vectorstore() respects USE_LOCAL_VECTORDB env flag
validate_ticker('AAPL') returns (True, 'Apple Inc.') using mocked or local EDGAR lookup
validate_ticker('INVALID') returns (False, '')
Text is correctly chunked (chunks ~1000 tokens, with overlap)
pytest src/tests/test_ingestion.py passes with mocked EDGAR API and mocked ChromaDB
No hardcoded API keys or file paths in the source code

phases.name: Phase 2a: LangGraph State Schema & LLM Factory
phases.description: Implement the foundational constructs for the multi-agent graph. In src/graph.py, define the TypedDict state schema with fields: empresa (str), ticker (str), riesgos_extraidos (list[str]), auditoria (list[str]), aprobado_por_humano (bool), reporte_final (str). In src/agents.py, implement the get_llm() factory function that returns OllamaLLM if USE_LOCAL_LLM=true, or ChatOpenAI/ChatAnthropic if false, reading model name from env vars. All functions must have full type hints.
acceptance_criteria: AriaState TypedDict is importable from src/graph.py
get_llm() returns correct LLM class based on USE_LOCAL_LLM env var
get_llm() raises a clear ValueError if neither local nor cloud is configured
pytest src/tests/test_state.py passes, verifying state field types and LLM factory switching

phases.name: Phase 2b: Agent Nodes (Risk Extractor, Compliance Auditor, Executive Summarizer)
phases.description: Implement the 3 core agent nodes in src/agents.py. Each node receives state: AriaState and returns a partial dict with only the keys it updates. (1) risk_extractor_node: uses get_vectorstore() + get_llm() via RAG to extract the top-5 financial, operational, or legal risks from the 10-K. Returns {riesgos_extraidos: list[str]}. (2) compliance_auditor_node: receives riesgos_extraidos, evaluates each risk against a simulated investment policy prompt, and flags high-severity items. Returns {auditoria: list[str]}. (3) executive_summarizer_node: takes approved audited data and produces a structured executive report. Returns {reporte_final: str}. All LLM calls must be traced via LangSmith.
acceptance_criteria: Each node function is independently callable with a mock state dict
risk_extractor_node returns a list of 5 risk strings (mocked LLM)
compliance_auditor_node returns a list of audit comments (mocked LLM)
executive_summarizer_node returns a non-empty string report (mocked LLM)
All nodes return only partial state (not the full AriaState dict)
pytest src/tests/test_agents.py passes with all LLM calls mocked via pytest-mock

phases.name: Phase 2c: LangGraph StateGraph Wiring & Human-in-the-Loop
phases.description: Wire all nodes into a compiled StateGraph in src/graph.py. The graph must include: a data_fetcher node (calls ingestion.py), then risk_extractor, compliance_auditor, an interrupt point interrupt_before=['executive_summarizer'], and finally executive_summarizer. Export a compiled graph object as `graph = workflow.compile(checkpointer=MemorySaver(), interrupt_before=['executive_summarizer'])`. Write an integration test that runs the graph up to the interrupt, verifies state, resumes it with update_state(), and confirms reporte_final is populated.
acceptance_criteria: graph.invoke({'ticker': 'AAPL', 'empresa': 'Apple Inc.'}, config) runs without error (mocked LLMs and EDGAR)
Graph pauses at interrupt_before=['executive_summarizer'] — confirmed via graph.get_state(config).next == ('executive_summarizer',)
graph.update_state(config, {'aprobado_por_humano': True}) successfully resumes execution
reporte_final is a non-empty string after graph completes
pytest src/tests/test_graph.py passes with full mocked pipeline

phases.name: Phase 3: LangSmith Observability & Faithfulness Evals
phases.description: Integrate LangSmith tracing and create an evaluation harness. (1) Verify LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true and LANGCHAIN_PROJECT=aria-10k are read from .env and that all graph node executions appear as traces in LangSmith. (2) In src/evals.py, create a LangSmith dataset named 'aria-10k-risk-evals' with at least 5 question/answer pairs about 10-K risk factors (e.g., 'What are Apple supply chain risks?'). (3) Implement a run_faithfulness_eval() function using langsmith.evaluate() with a Faithfulness criteria evaluator. The function must return average score and raise an alert if score < 0.85.
acceptance_criteria: Running the graph creates visible traces in the LangSmith project 'aria-10k'
LangSmith dataset 'aria-10k-risk-evals' exists with >= 5 examples
run_faithfulness_eval() returns a score between 0 and 1
Function raises ValueError or logs a warning if Faithfulness score < 0.85
pytest src/tests/test_evals.py passes with mocked langsmith.Client

phases.name: Phase 4: Streamlit UI, Real-Time Status & Human-in-the-Loop Gate
phases.description: Build app.py as the Streamlit frontend. Required components: (1) Sidebar: text input for ticker symbol, local/cloud mode selector via radio buttons, and an 'Analyze' button. The ticker symbol must be validated via validate_ticker(ticker) before executing the graph. (2) Main panel: pipeline status widget showing each node as Pending/Running/Completed/Error using st.status containers, updated via st.rerun() polling. (3) Human-in-the-Loop gate: when graph.get_state(config).next == ('executive_summarizer',), display the extracted risks and audit in a readable format (st.table or markdown), and show two prominent buttons: '✅ Approve & Generate Report' and '🔄 Reject & Re-evaluate'. Buttons must be disabled when the graph is not paused. (4) Final report panel: render reporte_final as structured markdown with a download button (.md format). (5) Disclaimer: visible banner — 'This analysis is for informational purposes only. It does not constitute financial or legal advice.' (addresses LEG-01, LEG-03).
acceptance_criteria: Streamlit app launches without error: streamlit run app.py
Entering 'AAPL' and clicking Analyze triggers the pipeline and shows agent status updates
Entering an invalid ticker shows a clear error message in the UI and does not trigger the graph
Approve/Reject buttons are only active when the graph is paused at the interrupt point
Clicking Approve resumes the graph and renders the final report in markdown
Disclaimer banner is visible on every page load
Mode selector (local/cloud) correctly sets USE_LOCAL_LLM and USE_LOCAL_VECTORDB at runtime
Download button produces a valid .md file with the final report content
